name: Friendship
shortDescription: Friendship in Sumerian mythology represents a profound bond, often elevated to a heroic and transformative level. It highlights loyalty, mutual support, and shared purpose, often exceeding societal expectations.
description: In Sumerian mythology, friendship is depicted as a powerful force that can shape destinies and even challenge the gods. It goes beyond mere companionship, embodying deep loyalty, mutual respect, and a shared commitment to overcoming adversity. The epic of Gilgamesh provides the most prominent example, showcasing the transformative power of friendship between Gilgamesh and Enkidu. Their bond, initially forged in rivalry, evolves into an unbreakable partnership, driving them to heroic deeds and ultimately shaping Gilgamesh's understanding of mortality and kingship. This friendship allows both characters to grow, Enkidu becoming more civilized and Gilgamesh becoming more compassionate and responsible. The loss of Enkidu profoundly impacts Gilgamesh, triggering his quest for immortality and highlighting the irreplaceable nature of true friendship. The themes of loyalty, shared adventure, and the impact of loss are central to the Sumerian understanding of friendship, demonstrating its significance in navigating the complexities of life and death. Sumerian texts emphasize the importance of choosing friends wisely and maintaining strong bonds, as these relationships provide support, guidance, and a buffer against the harsh realities of existence.

sources.title: Before Philosophy
sources.author: Henri Frankfort, H.A. Frankfort, John A. Wilson, Thorkild Jacobsen
